title: California Plans $200 Million EV Rebate Program After End of Federal Tax Credits
content: California Governor Gavin Newsom has proposed a $200 million rebate initiative to support electricâ€‘vehicle purchases after the federal government ended the $7,500 new EV tax credit and the $4,000 used EV credit. The state aims to revive incentives that previously operated under the Clean Vehicle Rebate Program, which concluded in 2023 after assisting hundreds of thousands of buyers. EV sales declined sharply following the federal programâ€™s expiration. Automakers are also adjusting plans, with Stellantis ending certain plugâ€‘in hybrid models and General Motors absorbing large financial charges related to EV investments.
author: Analysts Account
